staticCommands.name: cleanbranches
staticCommands.description: Delete older branches from a project.

options.description: An argument that provides the maximum age in months of a branch since its last activity and classifies it as old. Example: "--age 6" will delete all branches that have not had any commit activity in the past 6 months.
An argument that provides a regex blacklist pattern for branch names. All branches with names that conform to the provided regex pattern will be SKIPPED. All others will be worked on.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be deleted.
Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"
An argument that tells the program to only delete merged branches. All branches that have not been merged, will be ignored.
Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"
An argument that provides a regex whitelist pattern for branch names. All branches with names that conform to the provided regex pattern will be WORKED ON. All others will be skipped.

staticCommands.name: clone
staticCommands.description: An argument that tells the program to clone a GitLab repository.

options.description: Branch to switch to. Default on switching is the current local repository project branch. Default on cloning will be the default project branch (e.g. "master" or "main").
An argument that tells the program to clean all unstaged changes in the local repository.
Path where the locally cloned repositories should be located.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No folders will be created.
GitLab user email to use for cloning.
Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. If this value is not provided, it defaults to "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"
An argument that tells the program to reset and discard all current changes in the work tree before switching branch.
An argument that tells the program that the execution is running inside a pipeline. Cloning safe-guards will be disabled.
GitLab token to use for cloning.
GitLab user name to use for cloning.

staticCommands.name: consulkeys
staticCommands.description: Deploy Consul Keys to the machine from a provided JSON file.

options.description: An argument that points to the json file with content to deploy.
An argument that allows for the creation of a backup of all Consul keys to be overwritten.

Comma-separated list of forbidden key paths. Forbid the deletion and edit of all Consul keys starting with this path, so the program will not touch them. If key is not present in Consul it will be set regardless. Example: "coscine/global/db_name, coscine/global/gitlabtoken"
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No keys will be deleted, backed up, replaced or added.

An argument that tells the program to overwrite all keys inside Consul, while taking the whitelist into consideration. If this argument is NOT provided the program will only add missing keys inside Consul and existing keys won't be touched regardless of their value.

A redeploy argument which removes all Consul keys from your system and deploys them again. Touched are ONLY keys with a path contained in the whitelist. If the whitelist is empty on redeploy, ALL keys will be deleted. A backup will be forced.

Comma-separated list of allowed key paths. Limit the scope of the application only to the Consul keys starting with this path. Every other path not starting with any of the provided values will be IGNORED. Example: "coscine/global, coscine/local"

staticCommands.name: devbranch
staticCommands.description: Create a "dev" branch in every project.

options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual dev branches will be created.
Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"
Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"
An argument that tells the program to execute in update mode. All protected branches will first be unprotected and then protected again with the settings provided.

staticCommands.name: devrebase
staticCommands.description: Rebase target branch using the source branch. Should a rebase have conflicts, then a manual intervetion will be neccessary and the program will skip to the next repository.

options.description: Target branch name to rebase.
Path where the locally cloned repositories should be located. Local repositories are required to initiate rebase.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be created.
GitLab user email to use for cloning.
An argument that tells the program to execute git commands with the force flag.

Source branch name to use for the rebase of the target branch. Commits from this branch will land on the target branch. If no value is provided, will use the default project branch (e.g. master or main).
GitLab token to use for the GitLab client.
GitLab user name to use for cloning.

staticCommands.name: devsnapshot
staticCommands.description: Create new snapshot branch from the "dev" branch in every project.

options.description: An argument that points to the iteration to be used.
Name of the branch snapshot created from the "dev" branch (e.g. "Sprint/xxxx-xx").
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be created.
Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"
Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"

staticCommands.name: finrelease
staticCommands.description: Prepare the Release. This implies creation of Sprint branches and necessary Merge Requests.

options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to set as assignees.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests or branches will be created.
An argument that points to the iteration to be used.
An argument that tells the program to label all closed issues from an iteration.
Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to set as reviewers.

staticCommands.name: integrations
staticCommands.description: Generate the Integrator Script's "integrations.json" for the release.

options.description: Branch name to generate integrations.json for. If not provided, then it defaults to the latest project tag.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. Will only simulate a successful execution.
An argument that sets the output destination directory for the json file.

staticCommands.name: mergerules
staticCommands.description: Replace all existing Project Merge Rules inside GitLab with a new Merge Rule.

options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab group IDs to get Merge Request approval access.
Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to get Merge Request approval access.
Comma-separated list of GitLab groups. Will update the information in every project inside the groups provided. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend"
Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"
Approval rule title. Example: "Merging into master/main"
Amount of approvals required to be able to merge.

Protected branch name to apply the Merge Request rule to. It defaults to the default project branch (e.g. master or main). If this value is equal to "//ANY", the program will apply the rule to ANY branch in the project.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests rules will be created or deleted.

staticCommands.name: preprelease
staticCommands.description: Prepare the Release. This implies creation of Sprint branches and necessary Merge Requests.

options.description: Process all issues found in the iteration. Extract branch names automatically.
Comma-separated list of branches to be used.
An argument that points to the GitLab issue number to be used.
An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests or branches will be created.
An argument that points to the iteration to be used.
An argument that points to the Merge Request title to be used.

staticCommands.name: projectbadge
staticCommands.description: Create a "dev" branch in every project.

options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual badges will be created.
Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"
